title: Adaptive Markets and the 2008 coordination failure
summary: Andrew Lo's three-body market frame applied to 2008: rational models, behavioral herding, and fragile market structure coordinated destructively. Model-driven AAA ratings fueled herding, herding fueled leverage, structure hid counterparty risk, culminating in systemic collapse.
source: Ch04 — "Trading: Where Coordination Becomes Visible" (Lo, Raschke)

nodes.label: Market Structure
nodes.description: Shadow banking, opaque derivatives, weak oversight

nodes.label: Coordination Failure
nodes.description: Interbank freeze, Lehman, global financial crisis

edges.description: Models legitimize and scale complex exposure
Herding pushes more capital into the trade
Opaque structure permits hidden leverage to grow

identifiability_note: Models, psychology, and structure co-moved throughout the cycle; without an unshocked control economy the individual contribution of each body is not separable.
